bibliographic_data.title: Overlap Bias Matching is Necessary for Point Cloud Registration
bibliographic_data.abstract: Point cloud registration is a fundamental problem in many domains. Practically, the overlap between point clouds to be registered may be relatively small. Most unsupervised methods lack effective initial evaluation of overlap, leading to suboptimal registration accuracy. To address this issue, we propose an unsupervised network Overlap Bias Matching Network (OBMNet) for partial point cloud registration. Specifically, we propose a plug-and-play Overlap Bias Matching Module (OBMM) comprising two integral components, overlap sampling module and bias prediction module. These two components are utilized to capture the distribution of overlapping regions and predict bias coefficients of point cloud common structures, respectively. Then, we integrate OBMM with the neighbor map matching module to robustly identify correspondences by precisely merging matching scores of points within the neighborhood, which addresses the ambiguities in single-point features. OBMNet can maintain efficacy even in pairwise registration scenarios with low overlap ratios. Experimental results on extensive datasets demonstrate that our approach’s performance achieves a significant improvement compared to the state-of-the-art registration approach.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes an unsupervised Overlap Bias Matching Network (OBMNet) for partial point cloud registration, which addresses the issue of suboptimal registration accuracy due to insufficient overlap evaluation.
core_content.key_methodology: The method involves a plug-and-play Overlap Bias Matching Module (OBMM), comprising an overlap sampling module and a bias prediction module. The OBMM captures overlapping regions and predicts bias coefficients for point cloud common structures, which are then used in the weighted SVD for rigid transformation estimation. A neighbor map matching module is integrated to merge matching scores of points within the neighborhood.
outcomes: OBMNet achieves significant improvement over state-of-the-art registration methods
The approach can maintain efficacy even with low overlap ratios
core_content.full_text_markdown: Point cloud registration is a crucial task in robotics and computer vision, aiming to align multiple point clouds through appropriate rigid transformations. However, achieving accurate registration becomes challenging due to factors like occlusions, different viewpoints, and inaccurate scanning. To address this issue, the paper proposes an unsupervised Overlap Bias Matching Network (OBMNet) for partial point cloud registration. The OBMNet uses a plug-and-play Overlap Bias Matching Module (OBMM), which consists of two components: an overlap sampling module and a bias prediction module. These modules capture overlapping regions and predict bias coefficients, respectively. By integrating OBMM with the neighbor map matching module, the approach robustly identifies correspondences by merging matching scores within the neighborhood. Experimental results demonstrate that OBMNet significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in registration tasks across various datasets.
